issue.title: Dependency Graph v2 — visual upgrade pack: swimlanes, in-node progress fills, hover-to-trace lineage

successCriteria.description: Dependency graph renders horizontal swimlane bands behind nodes, with each band's color reflecting the dominant status of nodes at that dagre rank
Each IssueNode renders a thin progress bar showing phase progress (full green for completed/closed, partial blue for executing, empty for pending)
useDependencyGraph surfaces phaseProgress { current, total } from activeWork[issue:N].phase into IssueNodeData
Hovering a node dims non-lineage nodes and edges; ancestor + descendant chain stays full opacity
Hover lineage and the existing Critical Path mode coexist gracefully (lineage takes precedence on hover; critical path styling restores on hover-off)
All changes type-check cleanly via tsc -b (verified by inspection given the Windows pnpm reparse-point block) and existing tests continue to pass

phases.title: Status swimlane backgrounds at dagre ranks
phases.content:
Add a custom React component `<SwimlaneBackground>` rendered as a sibling to ReactFlow's `<Background>` inside the ReactFlow children of `DependencyGraph.tsx`. The component:

1. Reads laid-out node positions via `useReactFlow().getNodes()` (after fitView completes).
2. Groups nodes by their `position.y` value (each unique y is a 'rank' in dagre's layout).
3. For each rank, computes the dominant status from the nodes there (e.g., if 3 of 4 are completed, the rank is 'completed-dominant'; ties default to the higher-priority status: executing > failed > completed > pending).
4. Renders an absolutely-positioned `<div>` for each rank with a low-alpha gradient background scoped to the rank's vertical span.

Vertical band span = midpoint between this rank's y and the previous rank's y (top), midpoint between this rank's y and the next rank's y (bottom). For the topmost and bottommost ranks, extend to graph edges.

Color mapping (CSS custom properties, defined in DependencyGraph.css alongside the existing legend dots):
- executing-dominant: rgba(96, 165, 250, 0.06)
- failed-dominant: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.06)
- completed-dominant: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.04)
- pending-dominant: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.02) (subtle in dark theme)

Light-theme adjustments via `[data-theme="light"]` selector (lower alpha, shifted hues for legibility).

The component must NOT interfere with pan/zoom — render inside the ReactFlow viewport's transform so bands move with the canvas. ReactFlow exposes this via the `<Background>` slot; mounting alongside via the same pattern works.
verification: Open desktop app, navigate to a non-empty release in Dependency Graph view, confirm horizontal colored bands appear behind nodes
Toggle Critical Path mode on/off — swimlane bands remain unaffected by critical path styling
Pan and zoom — bands track with the graph (they don't stay fixed in viewport)
Bands have low enough alpha to not interfere with node legibility (eyeball test)

phases.title: Phase progress fill inside IssueNode
phases.content:
Surface per-issue phase progress as a thin colored bar at the bottom edge of each IssueNode.

Data plumbing (`useDependencyGraph.ts`):
1. Extend `IssueNodeData` (export from IssueNode.tsx) with `phaseProgress?: { current: number; total: number }`.
2. In `useDependencyGraph`'s node-building loop, derive `phaseProgress` from `activeWork[\`issue:${number}\`]?.phase`. If status is 'completed' or 'closed', use `{ current: total, total: total }` (full); if 'executing', use the live `{ current, total }`; otherwise leave undefined.
3. Pass into IssueNode's `data` prop.

Render (`IssueNode.tsx` + `IssueNode.css`):
1. Add a `<div className="issue-node-progress">` as the last child of the node container.
2. Inside it, a `<div className="issue-node-progress-fill">` with inline `style={{ width: progressPercent + '%' }}`.
3. CSS: 2px height, full width container with neutral bg, fill colored by status (green for completed, blue for executing). Position absolutely at bottom: 0 so it doesn't push title content.

No render when `phaseProgress` is undefined (pending/open nodes get an empty bottom strip — visual continuity, no jarring absence).
verification: After the v0.5.1 ship completes, the Dependency Graph view for a release with active executing issues shows in-progress bars partially filled
Closed/completed issues in the graph show fully-filled green bars
Pending/open issues show no progress bar (or a faint placeholder track)
Node title remains legible — progress bar sits below the title cleanly

phases.title: Hover-to-trace lineage
phases.content:
Add `hoveredId: string | null` state to `DependencyGraphInner`. On `<ReactFlow onNodeMouseEnter>`, set; on `onNodeMouseLeave`, clear.

Lineage computation (memoize on `[nodes, edges]`):
- Build forward adjacency map (source → Set<target>) and backward adjacency map (target → Set<source>).
- Provide a helper `getLineage(id)` that returns `{ ancestors: Set<string>, descendants: Set<string>, edgeIds: Set<string> }` via BFS in both directions from the given node id.
- Cache results by node id so re-hovers are cheap.

Apply styling (`styledNodes` and `styledEdges` useMemo blocks):
- When `hoveredId !== null`, compute lineage and apply: hovered node + ancestors + descendants render at full opacity; everyone else at 0.3 opacity. Edges on the lineage chain stay full opacity; off-chain edges drop to 0.15.
- Take precedence over Critical Path styling when both are active: lineage opacity overrides; critical path stroke color is preserved as a secondary signal on visible edges.

This lives in the same `styledNodes` / `styledEdges` blocks already used for critical path — just merge the opacity logic.
verification: Hover any node in the graph: ancestor + descendant chain stays bright; rest dims to ~30% opacity
Hover off (mouseLeave): full opacity restored cleanly with no flicker
Enable Critical Path mode, then hover a node not on the critical path: lineage wins (its chain is bright, critical path edges that aren't on the lineage chain get dimmed)
Disable Critical Path, hover again: works as in case 1
